title: Shorting the semis
question: Does conditioning a short on "overbought" semis (RSI>70, >15% above 200-DMA, +20%/21d) beat a blind short?
finding: No — every condition loses at every horizon (21/63/126-day mean −2.1% / −6.7% / −14.8%) and the edge over a blind short rounds to zero; the null replicates out-of-sample and on a second index. The deepest-loss setups are the *worst* shorts (melt-up risk), not a signal.
